title: Transcormer: Transformer for Sentence Scoring with Sliding Language Modeling

structured.summary: This paper proposes Transcormer, a Transformer-based model employing a novel Sliding Language Modeling (SLM) approach that enables efficient sentence scoring by leveraging bidirectional context in a single forward pass. The method introduces a triple-stream self-attention mechanism to prevent information leakage and demonstrates competitive or superior performance to existing causal and masked language models on multiple NLP tasks, with significant inference speed improvements.

strengths.point: Innovative single-pass bidirectional sentence scoring method
strengths.evidence: SLM estimates token probabilities conditioned on all other tokens except itself using a triple-stream attention mechanism, enabling sentence scoring with only one forward pass.

strengths.point: Strong empirical results across diverse tasks
strengths.evidence: Transcormer outperforms or matches GPT, BERT, and ELMo baselines on NMT reranking (BLEU), ASR reranking (WER), and linguistic acceptability (BLiMP), demonstrating broad applicability.
SLM requires only a single forward pass compared to MLM’s multiple passes, resulting in substantial speedups, especially on CPU, as shown in latency benchmarks.
Code and pretrained models are publicly available at https://github.com/microsoft/CyBERTron-LM/Transcormer.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparison to closely related bidirectional models
weaknesses.evidence: No conceptual or empirical comparison to models like XLNet or ELECTRA that also capture bidirectional context efficiently, weakening novelty claims.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient ablation and analysis of triple-stream architecture
weaknesses.evidence: No experiments isolating the contributions of forward, backward, and query streams or qualitative analyses such as attention visualization.

weaknesses.point: Missing standard intrinsic language modeling metrics
weaknesses.evidence: Evaluation omits perplexity or log-likelihood on held-out corpora, limiting assessment of intrinsic language modeling quality.

weaknesses.point: Incomplete experimental details and reproducibility information
weaknesses.evidence: Critical training hyperparameters, optimizer settings, and hardware/software configurations for latency measurements are not fully specified.

weaknesses.point: No analysis of scalability with sentence length or memory footprint
weaknesses.evidence: Lacks empirical studies on how performance and efficiency scale with sentence length or memory usage compared to MLM and CLM.

weaknesses.point: Limited discussion on generalization beyond English and sentence scoring tasks
weaknesses.evidence: No experiments or analysis on multilingual data, cross-domain robustness, or applicability to token-level tasks like sequence tagging.

questions.question: How does SLM compare empirically and conceptually to other efficient bidirectional models like XLNet or ELECTRA?
questions.motivation: Understanding relative advantages and limitations is crucial for assessing novelty and practical impact.

questions.question: What is the contribution of each stream in the triple-stream self-attention, and can simpler architectures achieve similar results?
questions.motivation: Clarifying architectural necessity informs design choices and potential simplifications.

questions.question: How does SLM’s inference speed and memory usage scale with increasing sentence length compared to MLM and CLM?
questions.motivation: Scalability is critical for real-world deployment on variable-length inputs.

questions.question: Can SLM be effectively adapted or extended to multilingual settings and token-level tasks?
questions.motivation: Broader applicability would enhance the method’s utility and adoption.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of comparison to closely related bidirectional models. No conceptual or empirical comparison to models like XLNet or ELECTRA that also capture bidirectional context efficiently, weakening novelty claims
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient ablation and analysis of triple-stream architecture. No experiments isolating the contributions of forward, backward, and query streams or qualitative analyses such as attention visualization
critique_points.point_003: Missing standard intrinsic language modeling metrics. Evaluation omits perplexity or log-likelihood on held-out corpora, limiting assessment of intrinsic language modeling quality
critique_points.point_004: Incomplete experimental details and reproducibility information. Critical training hyperparameters, optimizer settings, and hardware/software configurations for latency measurements are not fully specified
critique_points.point_005: No analysis of scalability with sentence length or memory footprint. Lacks empirical studies on how performance and efficiency scale with sentence length or memory usage compared to MLM and CLM
critique_points.point_006: Limited discussion on generalization beyond English and sentence scoring tasks. No experiments or analysis on multilingual data, cross-domain robustness, or applicability to token-level tasks like sequence tagging
